name: Mammals
description: Mammalian wildlife — facts, species, and behaviors

questions.q: What is the largest living mammal on Earth?

source.notes: Can reach 30 metres long and weigh over 180 tonnes.

questions.q: Which mammal holds the title of the world's tallest?

source.notes: Can reach up to 5.5 metres tall.

questions.q: What is the fastest land mammal?

source.notes: Top speed around 110–120 km/h in short bursts.

questions.q: What is the meat of a deer called in cooking?

source.notes: From the Latin 'venari', meaning 'to hunt'.

questions.q: A 'leveret' is the young of which animal?

source.notes: Technically a hare in its first year of life.

questions.q: A 'stallion' is specifically what?

source.notes: A young male horse is a 'colt'; a castrated one is a 'gelding'.

questions.q: A 'filly' is a young female what?

source.notes: Female horse under four years old.

questions.q: Which mammals are the only ones capable of true, sustained flight?

source.notes: Flying squirrels glide but don't truly fly.

questions.q: Which Australian/New Guinean mammal is famous for laying eggs despite being a mammal?

source.notes: Platypus and echidnas are monotremes — egg-laying mammals.

questions.q: What is the largest living rodent in the world?

source.notes: Native to South America; can weigh over 60 kg.

questions.q: A group of whales or dolphins is called what?

source.notes: Sometimes also 'school' for smaller dolphins.

questions.q: Which marsupial's pouch famously faces backwards — opening toward its hind legs?

source.notes: Protects young from dirt as the wombat digs burrows.

questions.q: A baby kangaroo, koala, or possum is called what?

source.notes: Used for most marsupials.

questions.q: How many cervical (neck) vertebrae does a giraffe have — the same as a human?

source.notes: Each vertebra is just massive — up to 28 cm long.

questions.q: Approximately how long is an African elephant's gestation period — the longest of any mammal?

source.notes: Approximately 22 months — about two years.

questions.q: Which mammal is famous for 'playing dead' when threatened?

source.notes: Gave rise to the phrase 'playing possum'.

questions.q: Hippopotamuses secrete a reddish substance often called 'blood sweat' that acts as what?

source.original_a: It Has A Clear Film That Protects Its Eyes
source.notes: Contains pigments that protect skin from UV radiation and bacteria.

questions.q: What famous carnivorous marsupial is native to the Australian island of Tasmania?

source.notes: The Tasmanian tiger (thylacine) went extinct in the 20th century.

questions.q: Bats navigate using what kind of sound-based sense?

source.notes: Emit high-frequency calls and listen to the echoes.

questions.q: What is the smallest mammal in the world by mass?

source.notes: Also called the 'white-toothed pygmy shrew'; weighs around 2 grams.

questions.q: Which mammal's tongue can be 45–50 cm (18–20 inches) long?

source.notes: Used for grasping leaves from acacia trees.

questions.q: A sloth belongs to the biological order Pilosa. Roughly how many teeth does a typical two-toed sloth have?

source.notes: Sloths have simple, peg-like teeth with no enamel and no incisors.
